mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly positive during rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain resilience, supporting higher corporate output and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative during periods of rising industrial production, or the signal fails to predict returns when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening labor demand and rising household income, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or payroll growth consistently coincides with negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 70% of high-inflation trigger instances, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations remain anchored below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth confirm synchronized macroeconomic demand strength, reducing recession probability and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent while nonfarm payrolls show month-over-month declines, the labor market enters a contractionary phase that suppresses consumer demand and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the dual-condition signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline in walk-forward validation.
